It has a distinct high-pitched, squeaky, and often frustrated or angry voice, exclaiming "It ain't Bendoville key!", making frustrated "Yaa-hoo!" shouts, and later taunting Bugs with "Mmm, that way!", "What's the matter, Bunny Rabbit? Speak to me! Why don't you say something?". Bugs refers to it as "George".

Book - VICTORY THRU HARE POWER.description: Orange-covered book with bold black text, opened to a page describing gremlins as a menace to pilots, featuring a black-and-white illustration of gremlins sabotaging a plane, from which Bugs Bunny reads aloud.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Bugs Bunny lounging comfortably, reclining on his back while reading a book titled 'VICTORY THRU HARE POWER' with a carrot in hand, all set to whimsical, mischievous orchestral music. He chuckles and laughs heartily, clearly amused by the content. The camera zooms in on the book's page, revealing a black-and-white illustration and text. Bugs, in his distinctive voice, reads aloud, "Get a load of this, folks. It says here, 'a constant menace to pilots are the Gremlins, who wreck planes with their diabolical sabotagy.'" He closes the book with a satisfied grin, then sits up, still holding his carrot, and begins to laugh again, exclaiming "Gremlins! Oh, murder! Gremlins! What a fairy tale!" followed by exaggerated, mocking laughter and "Little men! Oh, brother!" The whimsical music fades as a dramatic orchestral sting and metallic percussive clangs signal a scene change. The view shifts to a wide shot of a desert landscape with distant mountains under a yellow sky, where Bugs is now sitting atop a large, dark gray bomb, still munching his carrot. Suddenly, a small, blue-headed, yellow-bodied Gremlin Bird with red feet appears, dragging a massive wooden mallet, its heavy movement punctuated by "thump" sounds and the continued metallic clangs. Bugs watches with a mischievous, smug expression as the bird struggles, emitting grunting sounds, to pull the oversized mallet, emphasizing the slapstick humor.

In a desert landscape with green grass and distant reddish-brown mountains under a yellow sky, a bomb dropping sound is heard as Bugs Bunny, reclining atop a large dark gray bomb, mumbles before calmly explaining, "Please, blockbuster bombs, don't go off unless you hit them just right," then questioning and confirming, "Yeah? Yeah." Orchestral music begins as the Gremlin Bird, a small cartoonish bird, determinedly drags a massive wooden mallet across the ground toward Bugs, letting out a grunt of effort. Bugs, initially eating a carrot with distinct chewing sounds and a mischievous grin, reacts with exaggerated surprise and annoyance as the bird approaches. Bugs then stands up, addresses the bird with a mischievous "Hey, Mac!" and offers, "Let me take a whack at it." Dramatic orchestral music swells as Bugs grabs the mallet from the bird and swings it with exaggerated effort, mimicking a boxer’s stance, letting out a yell before exclaiming, "What am I doing?" He then drops the mallet, points dramatically at the carrot stuck in the ground, and performs a series of comical, exaggerated dance moves, including a high kick and a finger-pointing gesture, while singing a playful, taunting "Yoo-hoo" tune. The scene ends with Bugs looking directly at the camera with a wide, toothy grin, his eyes sparkling with mischief, as the Gremlin Bird watches in confusion. The 'Cartoon Classics!' logo appears in the bottom left corner, and '8thManDVD.com' is displayed in the bottom right throughout.

The scene opens with Bugs Bunny, a gray rabbit with long ears, white belly, and white gloves, reclining atop a large, dark gray bomb in a desert landscape, casually eating a carrot. He utters a surprised "Gasp!" and then speculates, "Hey, I bet that was a... Say, do you think that... Hey, could that have been a Gremlin?" A small, determined Gremlin Bird with a blue head, yellow body, red feet, and large eyes, drags a massive brown wooden mallet across the ground toward him, appearing comically strained. The Gremlin Bird, with a distinct high-pitched, squeaky voice, exclaims, "It ain't Bendoville key!" as it reaches the bomb and attempts to strike it with the mallet, producing a metallic clang. Bugs Bunny, unfazed, stands up, and with a sarcastic "What, did you hit the head, warm up?", grabs the mallet with exaggerated effort. He begins swinging it wildly with whooshing sounds, performing a series of comical dance moves, culminating in a loud "THWACK!" and "BOING!" sound, while the Gremlin Bird watches in confusion, making frustrated "Yaa-hoo!" shouts. Bugs then drops the mallet and strikes a pose, grinning mischievously at the camera, asking an unseen "George," "Which way did he go, George? Which way did he go?" The Gremlin Bird, now frustrated, replies with an annoyed "Mmm, that way!" Bugs sarcastically thanks it, "Well, gee, thanks a lot, George. Thanks a lot." A rapid, escalating whirring sound builds to a dramatic orchestral "WHOOSH!" and "BOING!" as the Gremlin Bird leaps onto Bugs’ head, where it begins to frantically pull at his ears and wiggle, causing Bugs to react with exaggerated annoyance. The Gremlin Bird taunts Bugs with "What's the matter, Bunny Rabbit? Speak to me! Why don't you say something?" before Bugs finally smirks triumphantly, responding with a confident "Uh-huh." The background remains a consistent desert scene with green grass, distant reddish-brown mountains, and a yellow sky, with the bomb and the gremlin bird’s mallet as central props, while the 'Cartoon Classics!' logo and '8thManDVD.com' are displayed in the bottom left and right corners respectively throughout the segment.

event_blocks.summary: A small, blue-and-yellow gremlin bird, emitting whirring, buzzing, and squeaking sounds, mischievously pulls at Bugs Bunny's ears while he lies on his back in a grassy field, prompting Bugs to exclaim "I like him. He's silly." Bugs then grabs a massive wooden mallet and springs to his feet as dramatic orchestral music swells. The scene shifts to a U.S. Army Air Field where a bomber begins to roar, and Bugs, now inside, dances wildly with the mallet to fast-paced music while the gremlin bird dodges the propeller and lands on the landing gear. As the plane taxis, emitting smoke, Bugs points and laughs at the gremlin bird flying past the window. The gremlin clings to the landing gear outside, and inside, Bugs continues his antics, punctuated by "OW!" screams. A loud crash is heard, followed by Bugs calling out "Ee, Gremlin!" and then "Nice Gremlin! Woo!" as the gremlin flies away, leaving Bugs alone in the plane, grinning triumphantly with the mallet. Later, inside the military bomber, the gremlin bird perches on Bugs' head, pulling his ears, to which Bugs responds with a chuckle. Bugs dances comically, then swings a large wooden mallet, causing the bomber to shake violently with mechanical sounds, while the gremlin dodges with "boing" sounds. The plane tilts, throwing Bugs into the air with a scream, and he lands covered in banana peels. The gremlin scurries away, leaving a trail of peels, and Bugs stands triumphantly. The bomber, marked with a blue star insignia and red-white-striped tail, dives sharply through a cityscape, its engine roaring. Bugs reappears, grabs the steering wheel from the gremlin bird, who had been at the controls singing "Noin' down!", and the plane performs wild maneuvers, narrowly missing a skyscraper. Bugs is thrown into the air again, tumbling through clouds, as the gremlin bird watches from the plane's wing with a mischievous grin. The scene concludes with the gremlin bird looking out a window, unbothered by the chaos, as the music ends with a mischievous sting. Throughout, "Cartoon Classics!" and "8thManDVD.com" are visible.

Military Airfield.description: Animated scene of a U.S. Army airfield with multiple aircraft, including a large bomber, parked on a tarmac under a partly cloudy sky, with hangars and a red-and-white checkered water tower in the background, accompanied by environmental sounds of plane engines and propellers.

Bomber.description: Large military aircraft with a blue star insignia on its fuselage, red-white-striped tail, and spinning propellers, shown inside with wooden paneling, a small window, and a bench, shaking violently and tilting as Bugs is thrown around, accompanied by propeller whirring, loud engine roars, and whooshing sounds as it dives through a cityscape, also making crash/impact sounds during its chaotic flight.

Mallet.description: Large, brown wooden mallet with a thick handle, initially dragged by the Gremlin Bird, then grabbed and swung by Bugs Bunny with exaggerated effort, producing repeated 'whoosh' and 'thwack' sounds, causing the plane to shake, and finally dropped as Bugs performs comical dance moves.

Bugs Bunny.description: Gray rabbit with long ears, white belly, and white gloves, initially stretched across the doorway in panic, then flung into the air, hanging upside down, and later grabbing the steering wheel, all while screaming in a high-pitched, panicked voice and displaying exaggerated expressions of distress, letting out a distinct 'Whaaa!' sound.

8thManDVD.com.description: Golden animated text with a glowing effect, appearing in the bottom right corner throughout the segment.

Gremlin Bird.description: Small, cartoonish bird with a blue head, yellow body, red feet, and large eyes, initially running past Bugs with light, scurrying footsteps, then dodging the propeller, clinging to the landing gear, perched on the wing, and finally looking out a window with a mischievous grin, speaking in a high-pitched, sing-song voice ('Noin' down!') and making chattering, almost laughing sounds.

U.S. Army Air Field Sign.description: Wooden sign with black text reading 'U.S. ARMY AIR FIELD', with fields for 'LOCATION', 'NO. OF PLANES', and 'NO. OF MEN' all marked 'CENSORED', and a large black sign below reading 'CENSORED!!' in white letters.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Bugs Bunny lying on his back in a grassy field, as a child-like voice states "He's five years old," which Bugs mimics. A small blue-and-yellow gremlin bird, making whirring, buzzing, and squeaking sounds, mischievously pulls at Bugs' ears. Bugs, initially relaxed, reacts with exaggerated annoyance and loud "OW!" screams, then declares, "I like him. He's silly." He suddenly springs to his feet to dramatic orchestral music and cartoon sound effects, grabbing a massive wooden mallet and swinging it with comical effort. The scene shifts to a U.S. Army Air Field, where a large bomber with a blue star insignia and red-white-striped tail is parked, its engine beginning to roar. Bugs, now inside the plane, dances wildly with the mallet to mischievous, fast-paced orchestral music, while the gremlin bird flies around, dodging the propeller and landing on the landing gear. The plane begins to taxi, emitting smoke from its rear, with distinct engine and propeller sounds. Bugs, still inside, looks out the window, pointing and laughing as the gremlin bird flies past. The gremlin then appears outside the plane, clinging to the landing gear, as the aircraft moves forward. Inside, Bugs continues his antics, dancing and gesturing wildly, punctuated by more "OW!" screams, while the gremlin peeks through the window with a sly expression. A loud crash sound is heard, followed by Bugs calling out, "Ee, Gremlin! Ee, Gremling, Gremling, Gremling!" and then "Nice Gremlin! Woo!" The scene ends with Bugs striking a triumphant pose, holding the mallet, as the gremlin flies away, leaving Bugs alone in the plane, still grinning mischievously. Throughout the segment, "Cartoon Classics!" and "8thManDVD.com" are visible in the corners.

The scene opens inside a military bomber, where Bugs Bunny, a gray rabbit with long ears, white belly, and white gloves, is initially seen lying on the floor, annoyed by a small, mischievous Gremlin Bird with a blue head, yellow body, and red feet, perched on his head and pulling his ears. Bugs lets out a signature "Eh, what's up, doc?"-like chuckle. The gremlin, possibly whistling, is visible through a small window, taunting Bugs. With a mischievous grin, Bugs stands up and begins to dance comically to upbeat, frantic cartoon music, swinging his arms and legs in exaggerated, rhythmic motions. He then grabs a large wooden mallet and swings it with great effort, producing repeated "whoosh" and "thwack" sounds, causing the bomber to shake violently with a mechanical whirring and grinding sound. The gremlin dodges the mallet with rapid "boing" sounds, flying around the cockpit, clinging to the landing gear, and narrowly avoiding the spinning propeller. Bugs, unfazed, continues his antics, performing a series of comical dance moves, including a high kick and a spin, while the gremlin zips around him. The plane suddenly tilts, and Bugs is thrown into the air, letting out a loud, high-pitched scream as he flips through the sky. He lands back inside the plane, now covered in banana peels, which land with "splat" sounds. The segment opens inside a military bomber, its propeller whirring, where Bugs Bunny is stretched comically across the open doorway, letting out a high-pitched, panicked scream. The Gremlin Bird, a small blue-headed, yellow-bodied creature, runs past him with light, scurrying footsteps, taunting Bugs by darting around the plane, dodging the spinning propeller, and clinging to the landing gear, all set to an upbeat, slightly mischievous orchestral cartoon score. Bugs, in a state of exaggerated distress, is flung into the air with a cartoonish "boing" sound and lands flat on the floor, still screaming. The plane then tilts violently, accompanied by dramatic music, as Bugs is thrown upward, hanging upside down from the ceiling, before being flung out of the plane and into the sky with a loud "thwack." The bomber, marked with a blue star insignia and red-white-striped tail, dives sharply through a cityscape of tall buildings, narrowly avoiding collisions, its engine roaring and whooshing. Inside, Bugs reappears, letting out a panicked, drawn-out "Whaaa!", and grabs the steering wheel from the Gremlin Bird, who, with a high-pitched, sing-song "Noin' down!" and chattering, almost laughing sounds, is now at the controls. The plane performs a series of wild maneuvers, including a near-miss with a skyscraper, punctuated by loud engine roars and crash sounds, while the dramatic music intensifies. Bugs is once again thrown into the air with a distinct "thump," tumbling through clouds, as the Gremlin Bird, now perched on the plane’s wing, watches Bugs’ chaotic flight with a mischievous grin. The scene ends with the Gremlin Bird looking out of a small window, seemingly unbothered by the chaos it has caused, as the music concludes with a mischievous sting. Throughout, the 'Cartoon Classics!' logo appears in the bottom left, and '8thManDVD.com' is displayed in the bottom right.

event_blocks.summary: The Thin Man and Rotund Man lounge on a raft, the Thin Man animatedly pointing at a hamburger while the Rotund Man remains unimpressed. After a brief exchange, the Rotund Man snatches and eats the hamburger, leading to the Thin Man's frustration. The Rotund Man then sprays the Thin Man's foot, eliciting a yelp and a "boing" sound, prompting the Thin Man to retaliate by pretending to eat the Rotund Man's foot with a knife and fork, causing the Rotund Man to wail. The scene shifts to a wider view of the raft in the ocean with a distant island, accompanied by dramatic orchestral music. The Thin Man uses an umbrella as a sail, shouting "LAND!" as the Rotund Man appears exhausted. The raft speeds up towards the island, with frantic orchestral music. Suddenly, a cartoonish explosion with orange and purple flames erupts, sending the Thin Man flying through the air, screaming, accompanied by whooshing and booming sounds. The scene transitions to a desert landscape where Bugs Bunny appears, holding a carrot and laughing mischievously, with smoke and debris rising behind him. The 'Cartoon Classics!' logo is visible in the bottom left corner, and '8thManDVD.com' in the bottom right. Later, the Thin Man screams "Food!" just before a chaotic explosion engulfs the scene, leaving behind debris and a smoking bomb, set to a tropical tune. Bugs Bunny emerges unharmed, asking "What's the good word, strangers?" He taunts the stunned Thin Man, now covered in red goo, and the exhausted Rotund Man with a comical dance and mock-Hawaiian speech. The scene concludes with Bugs Bunny's signature "Eeeehhh... what's up, doc?" sound as 'WHAT UP DOC?' text appears. In another segment, Bugs Bunny gestures in a tropical setting as text appears: 'NOW IS THE TIME FOR EVERY GOOD MAN TO COME TO THE AID OF HIS PARTY.' The Thin Man and Rotund Man react with confusion. The Thin Man questions, "Gee, did you say that?" as rapid mock-tribal chanting accompanies the text 'OFA ENO MAUA TE OFE POPAA.' Bugs Bunny and the natives mimic each other's movements. A book titled 'NATIVE CUSTOMS' is shown. The Thin Man drops a golden coin into a fiery pit, triggering an explosion that sends him flying. The Rotund Man eats a hamburger and sprays ketchup on the Thin Man's foot. The Thin Man, covered in red goo, points angrily at Bugs Bunny, who continues his dance. The segment ends with Bugs Bunny's laugh and 'WHAT UP DOC?' text. In a different scene, the Rotund Man, in a chef's hat, sings about cooking a rabbit in a cauldron. He presents a 'RABBIT' menu to the Thin Man. The Rotund Man attempts to serve a live Bugs Bunny on a spoon, but Bugs bites it. Bugs then leaps into the cauldron, causing a massive explosion that sends the Rotund Man flying. Bugs emerges from smoke, dancing joyfully, then is pulled into a cauldron by a rope. The Rotund Man stirs the cauldron as smoke billows. Finally, in a tropical setting, the Rotund Man eagerly anticipates a meal and presents a 'RABBIT' menu to the Thin Man. A roasted rabbit springs to life, controlled by strings, and dances. Bugs Bunny emerges from smoke, dances on a rug, and drops a golden coin into a fire pit, triggering a loud explosion that engulfs the Rotund Man. The Thin Man reacts with disgust, while the Rotund Man yelps. Bugs Bunny laughs. The scene ends with a wide shot of the tropical landscape, the Rotund Man uttering, "Oh, shucks!"

event_blocks.summary: The video commences with the Warner Bros. logo and the Merrie Melodies title card, accompanied by orchestral fanfares. A "Corny-Gie Hall Presents" sign flickers to reveal a poster for "A Corny Concerto," set to whimsical music. Credits for Robert Clampett, Robert McKimson, Frank Tashlin, and Carl W. Stalling appear with decorative swirls. A silhouetted conductor, revealed as a bald, bespectacled man in a tuxedo, energetically leads an orchestra against a warm gradient background, with "Cartoon Classics!" and "8thManDVD.com" logos visible. A male voice greets, "Greetings, music lovers!" and announces a waltz by "Johann Strauss." The scene shifts to a "TALES FROM THE VIENNA WOODS" title card, framed by trees and a castle, as a grand orchestral waltz begins. The setting transitions to a dark, eerie forest with gnarled trees and a misty sky. A pig-like Hunter in a red cap and tunic runs with a rifle, holding a sign "I'M HUNTING THAT RABBIT!!" A white dog with brown spots and a red collar lies panting, holding signs "RABBIT RUBBISH" and "DITTO," mocking the Hunter. The Hunter snorts as the segment concludes. The dog reappears in the eerie forest, holding "RABBIT RUBBISH" and barking. It then presents the book "IT AIN'T POLITE TO POINT!" by "EMILY POST." Bugs Bunny emerges, whistling and taunting the dog with a stick, causing exaggerated reactions. The Hunter, rifle in hand, hides behind a bush. A gunshot is heard, and Bugs Bunny startles the Hunter, asking, "Eh, what's up, doc?" The Hunter exclaims, "Oh, a rabbit!" and attempts to shoot, but Bugs Bunny dodges, grabs the rifle, and pulls the Hunter into a struggle, chuckling. The Hunter is flung into the air, landing on a tree branch, while Bugs Bunny laughs. The dog reappears with the "DITTO" sign. Another gunshot is heard, followed by repeated "Oh, a rabbit!" exclamations from the Hunter and chuckles from Bugs Bunny, ending with a final gunshot. The mysterious forest setting with deep blue and purple hues remains consistent throughout, emphasizing the characters' exaggerated expressions and slapstick humor.

The pig-like Hunter is heard snorting as the segment concludes, with the dark, moody forest backdrop consistently featuring deep blues and greens, contrasting with the bright red of the Hunter’s attire and the signs’ text, while 'Cartoon Classics!' and '8thManDVD.com' animated logos remain visible in the corners.

The scene opens in a dark, eerie forest with gnarled trees and a misty green-blue sky, accompanied by orchestral, slightly mysterious, and playful background music. A white dog with brown spots and a red collar lies on the ground, holding a sign that reads 'RABBIT RUBBISH' in bold red letters, repeatedly barking and growling. The dog then holds up a red book titled 'IT AIN'T POLITE TO POINT!' by 'EMILY POST', flipping it open to emphasize its message, continuing its barks and growls. Suddenly, Bugs Bunny emerges from behind a tree, making a whistling sound as he playfully taunts the dog with a long black stick, causing the dog to react with exaggerated surprise and confusion, its barks and growls intensifying. The scene shifts as a small, pig-like Hunter in a red cap and tunic runs through the forest, rifle in hand, and hides behind a bush. A gunshot rings out. Bugs Bunny pops up from behind the bush, startling the Hunter, and asks, 'Eh, what's up, doc?' The Hunter, in a squeaky voice, exclaims, 'Oh, a rabbit!' before trying to shoot him. Bugs dodges and retaliates by grabbing the Hunter’s rifle, pulling him into a comical struggle, chuckling 'Heh heh heh.' The Hunter is then flung into the air, landing on a tree branch, while Bugs Bunny laughs triumphantly with more 'Heh heh heh' chuckles. The dog reappears, now holding a second sign that reads 'DITTO' in red letters, continuing to mock the Hunter’s futile pursuit with barks and growls. Another gunshot is heard, followed by Bugs' chuckle and the Hunter's 'Oh, a rabbit!' again, then Bugs' chuckle and the Hunter's 'Oh, a rabbit!' once more, ending with Bugs' chuckle and a final gunshot. The forest setting remains consistent throughout, with deep blue and purple hues creating a mysterious atmosphere, and the characters’ exaggerated expressions and movements drive the slapstick humor.

event_blocks.summary: In a dreamlike forest with purple trees and a glowing blue ground, a Hunter, a spotted dog, and Bugs Bunny flee in panic, their fear turning to confusion as the music shifts to a playful circus tune. The scene changes to a surreal sky where the Hunter, now in a yellow bow tie, bandages a grimacing Green Rabbit, amidst sounds of struggle, grunts, and a high-pitched cry of pain, while the dog holds a 'FIRST AID' box. Subsequently, in a grassy field with purple trees, the dog carries the Hunter, who wears a blue cap and bow tie, as Bugs Bunny comically dances in a blue tutu to grand orchestral music. Finally, a bald Conductor in a tuxedo, with a lisp, gestures dramatically against an orange-yellow gradient, asking, 'Wasn't that wuv-we?' before announcing the next presentation, with 'Cartoon Classics!' and '8thManDVD.com' logos visible throughout.

event_blocks.summary: A bald conductor in a tuxedo energetically leads an orchestra, introducing "The beautiful Blue Danube" with a "pop" and laugh, before a "BLUE DANUBE" title card morphs into a swirling pond. Orchestral music begins as a stylized lake scene appears with a white swan and three cygnets gliding past lily pads. A mischievous black duck emerges from reeds, taunting the swan with "Quack, quack, quack!" and splashing water, eliciting an annoyed reaction from the swan. The duck gleefully laughs, continuing its taunts with comical expressions. Later, the black duck playfully splashes the swan, which retaliates by splashing water back with a branch. A silhouetted bird, revealed as a black-and-white duck with a red cap, dives into the water with a "whoosh." The scene shifts to the black duck humorously seasoning the swan with salt and pepper from shakers, accompanied by a "clink" and "sprinkle" sounds, causing the swan to flinch and swim away while the duck laughs maniacally. The environment transitions to a whimsical, cartoonish setting with the duck holding a large red fish, chasing the dazed swan amidst circus-like music and further quacks and splashes.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with the "Merrie" Melodies title card and the "Fresh Hare" title card, featuring Bugs Bunny, before transitioning to a snowy landscape where a 'WANTED' poster for Bugs Bunny is displayed. A rotund prospector, identified as a police officer, warns Bugs, "And we're, the long arm of the law is reaching out and closing in on you. You screwy rabbit!" The scene shifts to the same officer digging in the snow with a pickaxe, uncovering a carrot, which Bugs Bunny snatches from his hand with a mischievous grin, mockingly stating, 'Twat by his own twax.' The officer, frustrated, shouts, 'Come on out, you rabbit! Come on now, you into a west!' Later, the Prospector, chained to a bomb that ticks ominously, demands keys from Bugs Bunny, who playfully taunts him with a padlock and key before triggering the explosion. The Prospector, enraged, lunges at Bugs with a pickaxe, but Bugs dodges, causing the Prospector to fall into the snow. Bugs then taunts the Prospector, "On what charge? You got nothing on me!" The Prospector continues to accuse Bugs of various offenses, including "Resisting an officer, assault and battery, trespassing, disturbing the peace, miscellaneous misdemeanors, public nuisance, traffic violations," and more. Bugs retaliates by pulling the Prospector's hat over his eyes, causing him to fall into a snowdrift. As playful music begins, Bugs dances around the snow-buried Prospector, eventually taking his hat. The chase music resumes as the Prospector, now holding a rifle, pursues Bugs, who leads him to a snowman and taunts him, "Do you call yourself a mighty? You can't catch me! Why, you couldn't even catch a cold!" In an icy cave, Bugs punches the Prospector, then swaps places with him, leaving the Prospector holding the rifle. The chase continues with frantic music as the Prospector, out of bullets, yells, "Come on out of there, I'll fill you full of lead!" raw_segments: The segment opens with the iconic, upbeat orchestral cartoon theme music accompanying the "Merrie Melodies" title card, featuring bold white and red text with musical note accents against concentric red and black rings, crediting 'Produced by Leon Schlesinger in Technicolor'. This transitions to the "Fresh Hare" title card, showcasing Bugs Bunny in a top hat and cane with red cursive text, crediting I. Freleng, Michael Maltese, Manuel Perez, and Carl W. Stalling. The scene then shifts to a snowy, mountainous landscape, where the music takes a more dramatic turn as a 'WANTED' poster for Bugs Bunny, issued by the Mounted Police and declaring him 'DEAD OR ALIVE! (PREFERABLY DEAD)', is nailed to a tree. A rotund prospector, wearing a red jacket and black hat, appears, puffing his chest and pointing at the poster with a clenched fist. He speaks with a gruff, determined voice, warning, "And we're, the long arm of the law is reaching out and closing in on you. You screwy rabbit!" before stomping off-screen. The camera pans across the snowy terrain, revealing footprints, then returns to a close-up of the now slightly torn and fluttering poster. The music becomes more whimsical with a distinct clarinet melody as the scene quickly cuts to a new poster titled 'Read the Mounted Police Gazette', featuring a cartoon woman in a bikini. Throughout the segment, "Cartoon Classics!" and "8thManDVD.com" watermarks are visible in the bottom left and right corners, respectively.

The segment opens with an upbeat, mischievous orchestral tune as a close-up reveals a poster titled 'Read the Mounted Police Gazette,' featuring a cartoon woman in a bikini, setting a humorous tone. The camera pans down to a snowy landscape with footprints leading to a 'WANTED' poster nailed to a bare tree, which reads 'WANTED BY THE MOUNTED POLICE' with a sketch of Bugs Bunny and the phrase 'DEAD OR ALIVE! (PREFERABLY DEAD)' in bold letters. A rotund police officer, dressed in a red jacket, black hat, white gloves, and blue pants, appears, holding a rifle and following the footprints with determination. The orchestral music continues, becoming more suspenseful as he stops, looks around, and then begins to dig in the snow with a pickaxe, revealing a carrot stuck in the ground. As he pulls the carrot out, a distinct 'twack' sound is heard, followed by Bugs Bunny’s hand emerging from the hole, holding the carrot. Bugs Bunny himself then pops up, pulling the carrot out of the officer’s hand with a mischievous grin, and a voice, presumably Bugs', mockingly states, 'Twat by his own twax,' followed by a chuckle. The officer, startled, tries to grab the carrot back, but Bugs Bunny taunts him by pulling it away, causing the officer to stumble and fall backward in frustration. The officer's gruff voice is heard shouting, 'Come on out, you rabbit! Come on now, you into a west!' as the comedic orchestral music continues, emphasizing his humiliation as he is left empty-handed and defeated in the cold, snowy forest with distant purple mountains. Throughout the scene, 'Cartoon Classics!' and '8thManDVD.com' appear as animated text in the bottom left and right corners, respectively.

The scene opens in a snowy landscape, accompanied by dramatic, slightly ominous orchestral music and the sound of chains dragging and footsteps crunching in the snow. A rotund Prospector, dressed in a red jacket, black hat, and blue pants, grunts with effort as he drags a large black bomb, which emits a ticking sound, chained to his ankle. Visibly frustrated, he exclaims, "My keys! Where are my keys? My keys, my keys! My keys! This bomb will explode! Hurry up, give me the keys! Oh!" as he stumbles through the snow. Bugs Bunny then emerges from behind a tree, holding a carrot and a green padlock with a key. He smirks mischievously, twirling the key and padlock with exaggerated confidence, and calmly asks, "What's up, doc?" The Prospector, now enraged, demands, "Give me the keys! Quick! Get me out of this predicament! Hurry up and unlock these cuffs!" Bugs drops the carrot and padlock, revealing a small green keychain, and begins to dance playfully, taunting the Prospector while saying, "Alright, now, take this joint on. Take it easy. He's an excitable type." He then mutters, "Now let's see now, doc... the garage... the car... the front door... the back door..." as he searches for the right key, accompanied by a whimsical key jingle. With a triumphant "Ah, here it is!", Bugs triggers a loud explosion from the bomb. After a "boing" sound, Bugs calmly remarks, "Oh well." The Prospector, now furious, rushes toward Bugs, brandishing a pickaxe, and yells, "Oh, no you don't! You're under arrest!" He lunges at Bugs, who dodges with a comical leap, causing the Prospector to trip and fall into the snow. The Prospector grabs Bugs by the ear and shakes him violently, his face contorted in anger, while Bugs remains calm and unbothered, winking at the camera. Bugs confidently retorts, "On what charge? You got nothing on me!" as the Prospector continues to rage, concluding with a furious "Oh, no! Well, listen to this!" and a sound of a punch. The 'Cartoon Classics!' logo appears in the bottom left, and '8thManDVD.com' is watermarked in the bottom right throughout the segment, emphasizing the comedic contrast between the Prospector’s rage and Bugs’ nonchalance in the quiet, cold snowy environment.

In a snowy, wintry landscape with bare trees and distant purple mountains under a pale sky, the Prospector, wearing a red jacket and black hat, confronts Bugs Bunny, who holds a carrot and a green padlock with a key. The Prospector, his voice rapid and accusatory, shouts a litany of charges including "Resisting an officer, assault and battery, trespassing, disturbing the peace, miscellaneous misdemeanors, public nuisance, traffic violations," and continues with "Going for a boulevard stop, jaywalking, triple parking, conduct unbecoming to a rabbit, nice looking chap, violating traffic regulations." He then angrily grabs Bugs by the ear with a distinct pulling sound, pulling him close. Bugs, unfazed, winks at the camera and taunts the Prospector with a mischievous grin, calmly stating, "Why look at you. You call yourself a Mountie? You're a disgrace to the regiment. I'm gonna drum you out of the service," followed by a "thwack" sound. Frustrated, the Prospector drops the padlock and key with a clatter, then attempts to strike Bugs with a pickaxe, accompanied by quick, percussive sounds. Bugs dodges and retaliates by pulling the Prospector’s hat over his eyes with a "thump." The Prospector stumbles backward with a "whoosh," tripping over his own feet and falling into a snowdrift with a "thud." Playful, whimsical music begins as Bugs, still holding the carrot, dances playfully around the Prospector, who is now buried up to his waist in snow, accompanied by light "boing" sounds. The Prospector struggles to free himself, pulling his hat off with a "thwack" and throwing it aside, revealing his bald head. Bugs then grabs the Prospector’s hat and places it on his own head, winking again, as a triumphant musical flourish plays. The Prospector, now completely exasperated, stands up and lunges at Bugs, who dodges and runs away, leaving the Prospector to collapse in frustration with a final musical flourish. The scene ends with Bugs standing triumphantly, holding the carrot and the Prospector’s hat, while the Prospector lies defeated in the snow. The 'Cartoon Classics!' logo and '8thManDVD.com' watermark are visible throughout.

The segment opens with frantic, upbeat orchestral chase music as Bugs Bunny and the Prospector run through a snowy landscape, their shadows dramatically cast. The Prospector, in his red jacket and black hat, pursues Bugs, who taunts him with a mischievous grin. Cartoon sound effects accompany their running, and the Prospector trips and tumbles into a snowdrift with a comical thud. Bugs continues running, then stops to confront the stuck Prospector. The music shifts to a whimsical, Christmas-themed tune as Santa Claus emerges from behind a decorated Christmas tree, cheerfully exclaiming, "Merry Christmas, Teddy!" Bugs then playfully pulls a carrot from Santa's ear. The chase music resumes with renewed intensity as the Prospector, now holding a rifle, reappears and chases Bugs through the snow. Bugs leads him to a snowman, which he comically 'animates' by placing his hat on its head and winking at the camera. Bugs then taunts the enraged Prospector with a distinctive, chuckling voice, asking, "Do you call yourself a mighty? You can't catch me! Why, you couldn't even catch a cold!" He continues, "You know what I'm gonna do to you? I'm gonna punch...", before the audio cuts off, as Bugs dodges the Prospector, causing him to stumble and fall again amidst the consistent snowy environment. The 'Cartoon Classics!' logo and '8thManDVD.com' watermark remain visible.

The segment opens in a snowy landscape with an icy cave backdrop, where Bugs Bunny, a gray rabbit with a rifle, confidently taunts the rotund Prospector, declaring, "Right square in the nose," followed by a punch sound and a flash of lightning. Bugs vanishes in a puff of smoke, leaving the bewildered Prospector to groan in confusion. The Prospector stumbles, tripping over falling black rocks that create a crater with a thud. Frustrated, he kicks the ground, then notices a bomb chained to his ankle. He yanks it free, but is dragged backward by the rattling chain as the bomb rolls away. Furious, the Prospector runs after Bugs, who reappears near a tree, rifle in hand, and mischievously taunts, "Last one into... right knee." Bugs then swiftly swaps places with the confused Prospector, who now holds the rifle, and laughs as he runs off, dragging the Prospector behind him. A lighthearted, fast-paced chase music begins as the comical pursuit continues across the snow-covered ground, with the Prospector's legs flailing. The Prospector angrily shouts, "Come on out of there, I'll fill you full of lead!" and fires the rifle with distinct "Bang!" sounds, only to realize, "No more bullets!" with frustrated clicks, followed by a yell. The chase intensifies with frantic music and comical running sounds, as Bugs leads the Prospector rapidly through the snow, his red jacket and black hat visible against the white landscape, while the 'Cartoon Classics!' logo and '8thManDVD.com' remain visible.

event_blocks.summary: A snowy landscape with bare trees and distant purple mountains is introduced with dramatic, suspenseful orchestral music culminating in a loud 'BOOM!', as Bugs Bunny's shadow is cast. The Prospector, in a red jacket, approaches with a grin but trips over a rock with a 'thud', falling backward into the snow with a 'boing'. Bugs Bunny taunts the fallen Prospector, saying, 'Come on, buck up. Don't cry.' The grumbling Prospector, feeling 'squashed to a wedgement', struggles to rise and asks Bugs to 'take me in'. Bugs, with sarcasm, questions if he's serious, to which the Prospector angrily demands, 'No! Go ahead, snap 'em on!' A 'snap' sound accompanies Bugs pulling a golden bomb with a chain from the Prospector's ankle, comically dragging him forward, prompting the Prospector's resigned 'Okay, doc, let's go.' The scene transitions to a military encampment with a wooden palisade and stone building, now underscored by military-style marching music. Bugs, blindfolded and holding a rifle, faces a line of soldiers in red uniforms, while the Prospector, in a black hat and holding a sword, formally asks, 'Ready, Mr. Wabbit?' Bugs confidently replies, 'Yeah, go ahead, doc. I'm ready.' The Prospector offers, 'Before you die, you can make one last wish.' Bugs begins to ponder his wish with a drawn-out 'Yeah? Well, uh, let's see now, um... I wish, um... I wish... I wish...' before the segment concludes. The 'Cartoon Classics!' logo and '8thManDVD.com' are consistently visible with animated eyes and a golden effect.
